name: Talon Voice
description: Accessibility-focused voice control and dictation, popular for hands-free coding

developer.comment: Independent developer Ryan Hileman, known online as lunixbochs; funded via Patreon.

first-release.comment: Development began in 2017 after the author's RSI; Talon was in open beta (initially macOS-only, requiring Dragon) by early 2018. Exact first public release month is approximate.

status.comment: Actively developed; 0.4.0 line with ongoing beta releases as of late 2025.

license.comment: The Talon app/engine itself is closed-source (proprietary) with a free public release plus optional paid Patreon tiers. The community command set (talonhub/community) is open source, but that is separate from the tool.

primary-type.comment: Real-time voice control and speak-to-type dictation (hands-free input), not a file/meeting transcriber.

real-time-dictation.comment: Core function is live speak-to-type with low latency via the bundled Conformer engine.

file-transcription.comment: No documented audio/video file upload transcription; Talon is a live input tool, not a recording transcriber.

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting-bot or conference-recording capability; designed for controlling your own machine via microphone.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Recognizes speech in real time with notably low latency as you speak (live input), per independent review.

audio-sources.comment: Microphone-only live input; no file upload, system-audio capture, or meeting bot.

underlying-engine.value: Wav2Letter Conformer (bundled); also supports Dragon, Vosk, Webspeech
underlying-engine.comment: Bundled free Conformer (wav2letter) engine is recommended for new users; can also drive Dragon (via Draconity), and beta adds Vosk and Webspeech.

language-count.comment: As of April 2024 the main bundled Conformer model is English (en_US) only. More languages possible via beta Vosk/Webspeech or via Dragon, but the default engine is single-language.

english-accuracy.comment: Independent review: bundled Conformer is 'competitive with Dragon' with low latency, but 'accuracy still lags a bit behind Dragon' for disambiguating unclear speech. No published WER figure found.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Default Conformer model is English-only with no automatic language detection.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Bundled custom vocabulary system lets users add custom words via a simple file; works with both Conformer and Dragon.

speaker-diarization.comment: Single-user live dictation tool; no multi-speaker labeling.

timestamps.comment: Live input tool; does not produce a timestamped transcript document.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: No summary/notes generation; not a meeting/transcription product.

built-in-editor.comment: No in-app transcript editor; text is dictated directly into other applications, and voice commands handle correction/editing in-place.

export-formats.comment: Not applicable — Talon types text live into other apps rather than producing an exportable transcript file.

processing-location.comment: Bundled Conformer runs locally with no internet requirement. (Optional beta Webspeech engine is cloud-based, but the default/recommended engine is on-device.)

offline-capable.comment: Conformer operates locally without an internet connection.

data-used-for-training.comment: Default recognition runs fully on-device with no upload, so audio is not sent to the vendor for training. No formal privacy policy reviewed; rating reflects the local-processing architecture of the bundled engine.

data-deletion-control.comment: Not applicable in the usual sense — processing is local and Talon does not store a cloud recording/transcript library to delete; any local logs are under the user's own filesystem control.

platforms.comment: Cross-platform desktop app: macOS, Windows, and Linux (X11). No mobile or web version.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Dictation and voice commands work OS-wide across any focused application, not just within Talon's own window.

integrations.comment: No meeting/app service integrations (Zoom/Meet/Teams etc.); instead extensible via Python and .talon scripting to control any app.

api-automation.comment: Deeply scriptable via Python and .talon files, with a documented API for custom commands and automation.

pricing-model.comment: Free public release for all platforms; optional Patreon subscription unlocks early-access beta builds and priority support.

free-tier.comment: The full free public release (including the bundled Conformer engine) is genuinely usable indefinitely without payment.

free-allowance.value: Full free public release with bundled local Conformer engine
free-allowance.comment: No usage cap — local processing means unlimited dictation/voice control on the free tier.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Core use is fully covered by the free release, so entry price is 0. Optional Patreon support tiers start around $5/mo, with beta early-access at $25/mo (checked 2026-06-12).
